mnemonics: DETECT: Data Extraction, Transformation, ETL, Consolidation, Transformation. Represents the evolution from siloed operational data to a consolidated, transformed data warehouse.
keyPoints: Evolution started with operational databases used for daily transactions, leading to data silos.
The need for historical, integrated data for analysis drove the development of Data Warehousing (DW).
DW matured into full Business Intelligence (BI) systems, providing tools for reporting, analytics, and data mining.
commonMistakes: Believing a Data Warehouse is just a large database; it is a process-oriented architecture for integrated, time-variant, non-volatile data for decision support.
Confusing Operational Data Stores (ODS) for Data Warehouses; an ODS is integrated and current for operational reporting, while a DW is subject-oriented and historical for analytical reporting.
